mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer income, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year, real rates turn negative, incentivizing equity allocation as a hedge against currency debasement and producing positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 233.318 as of 2013-07
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered periods, or Rank IC of the interaction term drops below 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% signals deteriorating real estate demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST shocks fail to produce negative 5-day returns in >55% of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, signaling expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO acceleration fails to produce positive 20-day returns in >58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the signal drops below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline simultaneously by >3% month-over-month, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and real estate sectors. This joint signal predicts negative 20-day equity returns as forward earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines >3% AND HOUST declines >3% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 426,563M and HOUST at 896k as of 2013-07
failure_condition: The joint demand signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered periods, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as equity markets reprice recession probability.
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes fail to produce negative 60-day returns in >58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the signal remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%), real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized, signaling that monetary accommodation is successfully stimulating demand without inflation pressure. This regime-conditional mechanism produces positive 60-day equity returns as growth surprises to the upside.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and GDPC1 at 15,681B as of 2013-04
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with above-trend growth
failure_condition: The regime-conditional signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >62% of triggered periods, or Rank IC of the interaction drops below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, indicating stagflationary pressure with contracting supply and weakening labor demand. This interaction signal predicts negative 5-day equity returns as markets reprice stagflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines >0.3% AND UNRATE rises >0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 and UNRATE at 7.3% as of 2013-07
failure_condition: The stagflation interaction signal fails to produce negative 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
